  "findings": [
    "Repetitive, sharp impact-like clicking and tapping sounds indicating a localized defect on a rolling element or race",
    "High-frequency transient ringing following each impact, characteristic of metal-on-metal contact in a damaged bearing"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Schedule immediate bearing replacement during the next planned maintenance window",
    "Monitor bearing temperature and vibration levels closely until replacement is executed",
    "Check lubrication for contamination or metal shavings"
  ],
  "rationale": "The audio recording reveals distinct, periodic impact-like clicking sounds superimposed on the background noise of the slow-turning conveyor drive. This pattern is highly characteristic of a localized defect (such as spalling, pitting, or a crack) on the bearing's rolling elements or raceways, indicating a worn and degraded component that requires replacement to prevent catastrophic failure.",
  "references": [
    "ISO 13373 (Condition monitoring and diagnostics of machines)",
    "SKF Bearing Damage and Failure Analysis Guide"
  ]
}
